title: Intricate Mustardy French Cream Eclair
description: A sugar-free friendly French eclair with a mustardy twist, featuring black cardamom pods and pickle, perfect for a brunch mood, this dish combines the flavors of Lyon with a unique creamy filling.
instructions: Preheat the oven to 400 degrees Fahrenheit.
In a medium saucepan, combine the milk, melted butter, and ground black cardamom pods, and heat over medium heat until the mixture is hot but not boiling.
In a small bowl, whisk together the egg yolks and Dijon mustard until well combined.
Temper the egg yolk mixture by slowly pouring the warm milk mixture into the eggs, whisking constantly.
Add the pickle relish and whisk until smooth.
In a separate bowl, sift the almond flour.
Add the sifted almond flour to the egg yolk mixture and whisk until well combined.
Transfer the mixture to a piping bag fitted with a large round tip.
Pipe the mixture into long, thin strips onto a parchment-lined baking sheet.
Bake for 15 minutes, then reduce the oven temperature to 350 degrees Fahrenheit and bake for an additional 10 minutes.
Allow the eclairs to cool completely on a wire rack.
Fill the cooled eclairs with whipped heavy cream and serve immediately.
Note: this recipe is for refrigerator pickling/preserving only — store in the refrigerator and consume within 1 week. Do not water-bath can without an acid (vinegar or lemon juice).
